     .with_decorations(false)
+    // There are actually three layer of background color when creating webview window.
+    // The first is window background...
     .with_transparent(true)
     .build(&event_loop)
     .unwrap();

+        // Transparent

         settings.put_is_status_bar_enabled(false)?;

results.title: Transparency not working on Windows 11 · Issue #1026 · tauri-apps/wry
results.author: UmGarcia
results.text:
# Issue: tauri-apps/wry #1026

- Repository: tauri-apps/wry | Cross-platform WebView library in Rust for Tauri. | 5K stars | Rust

## Transparency not working on Windows 11

- Author: [@UmGarcia](https://github.com/UmGarcia)
- State: closed (not_planned)
- Labels: type: bug
- Created: 2023-09-16T15:56:02Z
- Updated: 2023-09-16T22:30:19Z
- Closed: 2023-09-16T22:21:21Z
- Closed by: [@amrbashir](https://github.com/amrbashir)

Using title bar example code and others codes the transparency don't work on windows 11 using version 0.33.0

[Image: image | https://github.com/tauri-apps/wry/assets/8272623/1fed5b98-ba3c-4b22-9f1c-23369e8326a2]

When I maximize and minimize the window it becomes transparent, but with borders:

[Image: image | https://github.com/tauri-apps/wry/assets/8272623/d7cf10d1-b56e-4956-a7ee-24c26487a74b]

When using version 0.24.4 the screen respects transparency:

[Image: image | https://github.com/tauri-apps/wry/assets/8272623/134864e2-4a0d-4e2d-93a1-a0098c4df685]

I'm new using WRY and Rust, so I have no idea what the problem could be, but I validated that all versions above 0.24.4 are not complying with transparency in Windows 11.

**Would you want to assign yourself to resolve this bug?**

- [ ] Yes
- [ ] No

---

### Timeline

**UmGarcia** added label `type: bug` · Sep 16, 2023 at 3:56pm

**UmGarcia** changed the title from "Transparent not working on Windows 11" to "Transparency not working on Windows 11" · Sep 16, 2023 at 4:21pm

**@amrbashir** commented · Sep 16, 2023 at 10:21pm

> this is because the shadows are turned on when the window is created, so you need to call https://docs.rs/wry/latest/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/wry/application/window/struct.WindowBuilder.html#method.with_undecorated_shadow to disable it while creating the window then enable it back (if you want) using https://docs.rs/wry/latest/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/wry/application/window/struct.Window.html#method.set_undecorated_shadow
> 
> See this example https://github.com/tauri-apps/window-vibrancy/blob/ce6e299a3bc98bd1d0f322c3b922fe8634f09e8e/examples/winit.rs#L21-L36

**amrbashir** closed this · Sep 16, 2023 at 10:21pm

**@UmGarcia** commented · Sep 16, 2023 at 10:30pm · Author

> > this is because the shadows are turned on when the window is created, so you need to call https://docs.rs/wry/latest/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/wry/application/window/struct.WindowBuilder.html#method.with_undecorated_shadow to disable it while creating the window then enable it back (if you want) using https://docs.rs/wry/latest/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/wry/application/window/struct.Window.html#method.set_undecorated_shadow
> > 
> > See this example https://github.com/tauri-apps/window-vibrancy/blob/ce6e299a3bc98bd1d0f322c3b922fe8634f09e8e/examples/winit.rs#L21-L36
> 
> I'm extremely grateful for that clarification, I didn't imagine that shadowing enabled or not in version 0.24.4 doesn't occur.
> 
> I just tested it and now it is as expected.
> 
> Thank you!

**nicholasc** mentioned this in issue [#1212: Windows 11 Transparency goes through wgpu example](https://github.com/tauri-apps/wry/issues/1212) · Apr 2, 2024 at 11:57pm

[bug] Transparent attribute on child window is not taking effect · Issue #12450 · tauri-apps/tauri
results.author: tnthung
results.text:
# Issue: tauri-apps/tauri #12450 - Repository: tauri-apps/tauri | Build smaller, faster, and more secure desktop and mobile applications with a web frontend. | 105K stars | Rust ## [bug] Transparent attribute on child window is not taking effect - Author: [@tnthung](https://github.com/tnthung) - State: closed (completed) - Labels: type: bug, status: needs triage - Created: 2025-01-20T05:20:36Z - Updated: 2025-01-22T06:59:34Z - Closed: 2025-01-22T06:59:33Z - Closed by: [@tnthung](https://github.com/tnthung) ### Describe the bug Im trying to embed the tauri webview into my experimental bevy project. I've managed to make the tauri spawn a child window in the bevy window, but the `WebviewWindowBuilder::transparent` does not correctly make the it transparent. What I've imagining is to have the tauri as a transparent overlay on top of bevy render, but the result is either - When `transparent(false) / not set` The webview background is pure white. [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38f68d81-33f7-4b54-8d43-c6fbe4a7eb3f] - When `transparent(true)` The webview background is visually pure black. [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f686928-6f38-4ca1-b101-aa1548aba408] I've set the css bgc for whole html element to be `#0000`, and the inspector does say the color is `#0000`. But it's not actually letting things behind to pass-through. [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/625b4c53-253d-4dcb-b951-2d4cb0a05701] If remove the parenting, the transparency worked as expected, but the `bevy` window and `tauri` window are now independent and way harder to manage. [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea076c6d-441a-493a-8f8e-e59d8b96bada] Is there anything I overlooked? ### Reproduction 1. Create a empty tauri project. 1. CD into `src-tauri`, `cargo add` both `bevy` and `wry`. 1. Paste following code into lib.rs ```rs use bevy::prelude::*; use bevy::winit::WinitWindows; use bevy::window::PrimaryWindow; use wry::raw_window_handle::{HasWindowHandle, RawWindowHandle}; #[cfg_attr(mobile, tauri::mobile_entry_point)] pub fn run() { App::new() .add_plugins(DefaultPlugins) .add_systems(PostStartup, init_tauri) .run(); } fn init_tauri( winit_windows: NonSend, window: Single>, ) { let window = winit_windows .get_window(window.into_inner()) .unwrap(); let RawWindowHandle::Win32(handle) = window.window_handle().unwrap().as_raw() else { println!("not windows window"); return; }; let tmp = handle.hwnd; std::thread::spawn(move || { tauri::Builder::default() .plugin(tauri_plugin_opener::init()) .any_thread() .invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![]) .setup(move |app| { use tauri::*; WebviewWindowBuilder::new(app, "main", WebviewUrl::App("index.html".into())) .parent_raw(unsafe { std::mem::transmute(tmp) }) .inner_size(300., 200.) .decorations(false) .transparent(true) // not working as expected .position(0., 0.) .shadow(false) .resizable(false) .closable(false) .build()?; Ok(()) }) .run(tauri::generate_context!()) .expect("error while running tauri application"); }); } ``` 1. Add any html for testing and make sure the `background-color` property is set to something transparent. Mine: ```html // `app.html` Tauri + SvelteKit + Typescript App %sveltekit.head%
%sveltekit.body%
``` ```html // the root `+page.svelte`

Page

This is a page

``` 1. `npm tauri dev` ### Expected behavior The embeded child window should be transparent and letting the things behind to be visible in the viewport. ### Full `tauri info` output ```text > the-world@0.1.0 tauri > tauri info [✔] Environment - OS: Windows 10.0.26100 x86_64 (X64) ✔ WebView2: 131.0.2903.146 ✔ MSVC: Visual Studio Community 2019 ✔ rustc: 1.83.0 (90b35a623 2024-11-26) ✔ cargo: 1.83.0 (5ffbef321 2024-10-29) ✔ rustup: 1.27.1 (54dd3d00f 2024-04-24) ✔ Rust toolchain: stable-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (default) - node: 21.6.0 - pnpm: 9.12.1 - npm: 10.2.4 [-] Packages - tauri 🦀: 2.2.3 - tauri-build 🦀: 2.0.5 - wry 🦀: 0.48.1 - tao 🦀: 0.31.1 - @tauri-apps/api : 2.2.0 - @tauri-apps/cli : 2.2.5 [-] Plugins - tauri-plugin-opener 🦀: 2.2.5 - @tauri-apps/plugin-opener : 2.2.5 [-] App - build-type: bundle - CSP: unset - frontendDist: ../build - devUrl: http://localhost:1420/ - framework: Svelte - bundler: Vite ``` ### Stack trace ```text ``` ### Additional context _No response_ --- ### Timeline **tnthung** added label `status: needs triage`; added label `type: bug` · Jan 20, 2025 at 5:20am **@tnthung** commented · Jan 21, 2025 at 4:45am · Author > The black color of child window background seems coming from the `WebviewWindowBuilder::background_color`. When I set the background color to something non-black, the child window's background changed, but it completely ignore the alpha channel, and does not respect the `WebviewWindowBuilder::transparent` setting either. **@tnthung** commented · Jan 22, 2025 at 6:59am · Author > This is related to `WS_CLIPCHILDREN` window attribute of the parent window. At current bevy version `0.15.1` is defaulted to SET, thus even if the tauri window does transparent, it'll still be clipped by the parent. This has been solved in https://github.com/bevyengine/bevy/issues/16544. > > ```diff > App::new() > - .add_plugins(DefaultPlugins) > + .add_plugins(DefaultPlugins > + .set(WindowPlugin { > + primary_window: Some(Window { > + clip_children: false, > + ..Default::default() > + }), > + ..Default::default() > + })) > .add_systems(PostStartup, init_tauri) > ``` > > With this minor modification, the tauri window can now be transparent with no problem. **tnthung** closed this · Jan 22, 2025 at 6:59am
highlights:

[...]
Im trying to embed the tauri webview into my experimental bevy project. I've managed to make the tauri spawn a child window in the bevy window, but the `WebviewWindowBuilder::transparent` does not correctly make the it transparent. What I've imagining is to have the tauri as a transparent overlay on top of bevy render, but the result is either
[...]
- When `transparent(false) / not set`

The webview background is pure white.
[...]

[...]

The webview background is visually pure black.

[Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f686928-6f38-4ca1-b101-aa1548aba408]
[...]
I've set the css bgc for whole html element to be `#0000`, and the inspector does say the color is `#0000`. But it's not actually letting things behind to pass-through.

[Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/625b4c53-253d-4dcb-b951-2d4cb0a05701]
[...]
If remove the parenting, the transparency worked as expected, but the `bevy` window and `tauri` window are now independent and way harder to manage.
[...]
[Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea076c6d-441a-493a-8f8e-e59d8b96bada]
[...]
```rs
use bevy::prelude::*;
use bevy::winit::WinitWindows;
use bevy::window::PrimaryWindow;
use wry::raw_window_handle::{HasWindowHandle, RawWindowHandle};
[...]
fn init_tauri(
  winit_windows: NonSend<WinitWindows>,
  window: Single<Entity, With<PrimaryWindow>>,
) {
  let window = winit_windows
    .get_window(window.into_inner())
    .unwrap();

  let RawWindowHandle::Win32(handle) = window.window_handle().unwrap().as_raw()
    else { println!("not windows window"); return; };

  let tmp = handle.hwnd;

  std::thread::spawn(move || {
    tauri::Builder::default()
      .plugin(tauri_plugin_opener::init())
      .any_thread()
      .invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![])
      .setup(move |app| {
        use tauri::*;

        WebviewWindowBuilder::new(app, "main", WebviewUrl::App("index.html".into()))
          .parent_raw(unsafe { std::mem::transmute(tmp) })
          .inner_size(300., 200.)
          .decorations(false)
          .transparent(true)     // not working as expected
          .position(0., 0.)
          .shadow(false)
          .resizable(false)
          .closable(false)
          .build()?;

        Ok(())
      })
      .run(tauri::generate_context!())
      .expect("error while running tauri application");
  });
}
[...]
1. Add any html for testing and make sure the `background-color` property is set to something transparent.
[...]
The embeded child window should be transparent and letting the things behind to be visible in the viewport.
[...]
[✔] Environment
    - OS: Windows 10.0.26100 x86_64 (X64)
    ✔ WebView2: 131.0.2903.146
    ✔ MSVC: Visual Studio Community 2019
    ✔ rustc: 1.83.0 (90b35a623 2024-11-26)
    ✔ cargo: 1.83.0 (5ff
[...]

> The black color of child window background seems coming from the `WebviewWindowBuilder::background_color`. When I set the background color to something non-black, the child window's background changed, but it completely ignore the alpha channel, and does not respect the `WebviewWindowBuilder::transparent` setting either.
[...]
> This is related to `WS_CLIPCHILDREN` window attribute of the parent window. At current bevy version `0.15.1` is defaulted to SET, thus even if the tauri window does transparent, it'll still be clipped by the parent. This has been solved in https://github.com/bevyengine/bevy/issues/16544.
> 
> ```diff
> App::new()
> - .add_plugins(DefaultPlugins)
> + .add_plugins(DefaultPlugins
> + .set(WindowPlugin {
> + primary_window: Some(Window {
> + clip_children: false,
> + ..Default::default()
> + }),
> + ..Default::default()
> + }))
> .add_systems(PostStartup, init_tauri)
> ```
> 
> With this minor modification, the tauri window can now be transparent with no problem.

results.title: RyanAhmed2025/RYTHON
results.text:
# Repository: RyanAhmed2025/RYTHON

Python Frameless Webview Template built for RYNOTE

- Stars: 1
- Forks: 0
- Watchers: 1
- Open issues: 0
- Primary language: Python
- Languages: Python
- License: Other (NOASSERTION)
- Topics: flask-application, frameless-python, frameless-window, python, python-container, ui-design, webview, webview-app
- Default branch: main
- Created: 2026-03-25T08:13:11Z
- Last push: 2026-03-25T08:49:43Z
- Contributors: 1 (top: RyanAhmed2025)

---

# RYTHON

> **A frameless Python desktop app template** — Flask backend · pywebview shell · WinAPI window control · zero Electron.

 

## Screenshots

| Light Mode | Dark Mode |
| ![Light Mode](screenshots/light.png) | Note: the Traffic Button colors are only visible upon Hover as demonstrated above
| ![Dark Mode](screenshots/dark.png) |
 
| Confirmation Dialog |
| ![Confirmation Dialog](screenshots/dialog.png) |
 

---

## What is Rython?

Rython is a **single-file fullstack desktop app template** written in Python. It lets you build a native-feeling Windows desktop application using nothing but a Flask server, a pywebview shell, and a hand-rolled WinAPI layer — no Electron, no Node.js, no browser chrome.

The entire UI lives in HTML/CSS/JS served locally by Flask on `127.0.0.1:5000`. The window itself is a chromium webview with `frameless=True`, dressed up with a custom title bar, resize handles, snap shortcuts, collapsible sidebars, a macOS-style dialog system, and a persistent dark/light theme — all implemented without a single native UI framework.

---

## The Frameless Window Problem — and How Rython Solves It

Frameless windows in pywebview have a well-known limitation: removing the OS frame also strips the native **resize hit-testing**, **drag behaviour**, and **snap-to-edge** support that Windows provides for free on decorated windows. Most projects either live with this (no resizing) or bolt on a heavy workaround that breaks on multi-monitor setups or high-DPI displays.

Rython solves this with a lean, direct WinAPI approach:

- **Resize handles** — 8 px invisible CSS strips at every edge and corner. On `mousedown` they call `window.pywebview.api.start_drag(mode)`, which issues a `SendMessage(hwnd, WM_NCLBUTTONDOWN, HT_*)` hit-test directly into the window message queue. Windows then handles the resize natively — no JS polling, no coordinate math, no jitter.
- **Title-bar drag** — a daemon thread polls `GetAsyncKeyState` at 5 ms intervals and calls `SetWindowPos` in a tight loop only while the left mouse button is held. This keeps the UI thread completely free and produces smooth, lag-free drag.
- **Snap shortcuts** — `Alt+←`, `Alt+→`, `Alt+↑`, `Alt+↓` call `snap_window()`, which queries the correct monitor's work area via `MonitorFromPoint` / `GetMonitorInfoW` and clamps the window to the target bounds — correctly handling taskbars, multi-monitor layouts, and different DPI scales.
- **HWND cache** — the window handle is resolved once via `FindWindowW` and cached, so every subsequent WinAPI call is a direct pointer dereference with no search overhead.
- **Style preservation** — the original `WS_*` style flags are saved at startup and restored before any `SetWindowPos` call, preventing the invisible-border artefact that appears when pywebview's internal style patching conflicts with a resize operation.

The net result is a frameless window that behaves identically to a native one: resizable from all edges and corners, draggable, snappable, maximisable, and correctly clipped to the monitor work area — without any visible frame, title bar chrome, or OS decoration.

---

## Features

**Window & chrome**

- Frameless window with fully custom title bar
- Resize from all 8 edges and corners via WinAPI hit-testing
- Title-bar drag via daemon-thread `SetWindowPos` loop
- Snap: `Alt+←` left-half · `Alt+→` right-half · `Alt+↓` centred · `Alt+↑` maximise ↔ restore
- Double-click title bar to maximise / restore
- Traffic-light close and minimise buttons

**Navigation**

- Left pill: 4 fixed + 3 hover-revealed icon buttons
- Right pill: 3 hover (including theme toggle) + 3 fixed + traffic lights
- Collapsible sidebars via `ArrowLeft` / `ArrowRight`
- Sidebar borders fade seamlessly into the title bar
- Centre title pill: app name + rolling status line

**UX**

- Unibar command overlay — `Alt+S` or double-click title → `//commands`
- macOS-style modal dialogs: `showDialog(msg, icon?)` and `showInputDialog(msg, default?)`
- Dark / light theme persisted to `localStorage`
- SQLite ready — commented scaffold for `get_conn()`, `init_db()`, and full CRUD routes
- `WindowApi` class exposes OS-level methods to JS: file/folder dialogs, window control, extensible

**Developer ergonomics**

- Single `.py` file — no build step, no bundler, no config files to wrangle
- Inline HTML/CSS/JS — everything in one place during prototyping; split to `static/` and `templates/` when scaling
- Commented project layout guide for growing beyond one file
- PyInstaller packaging to a single `.exe` with one command
- Zero required dependencies beyond `flask` and `pywebview`

---

## Quickstart

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python rython.py
```

To package to a standalone executable:

```bash
pip install pyinstaller
pyinstaller --noconsole --onefile --name RYTHON rython.py
```

---

## Project Layout (when scaling up)

```
rython/
├── app.py              ← Flask backend (keep lean)
├── database.py         ← DB init, get_conn(), migrations
├── models.py           ← Pure-Python data classes / business logic
├── routes/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── items.py        ← @items_bp.route('/api/items', ...)
├── static/
│   ├── app.js
│   ├── style.css
│   └── icons/
├── templates/
│   └── index.html
├── rython.db           ← auto-created; add to .gitignore
├── .env                ← secrets — NEVER commit
├── .gitignore
└── requirements.txt
```

---

*Built by Ryan Ahmed*
highlights:
> **A frameless Python desktop app template** — Flask backend · pywebview shell · WinAPI window control · zero Electron.
[...]
Rython is a **single-file fullstack desktop app template** written in Python. It lets you build a native-feeling Windows desktop application using nothing but a Flask server, a pywebview shell, and a hand-rolled WinAPI layer — no Electron, no Node.js, no browser chrome.
[...]
The entire UI lives in HTML/CSS/JS served locally by Flask on `127.0.0.1:5000`. The window itself is a chromium webview with `frameless=True`, dressed up with a custom title bar, resize handles, snap shortcuts, collapsible sidebars, a macOS-style dialog system, and a persistent dark/light theme — all implemented without a single native UI framework.
[...]
Frameless windows in pywebview have a well-known limitation: removing the OS frame also strips the native **resize hit-testing**, **drag behaviour**, and **snap-to-edge** support that Windows provides for free on decorated windows. Most projects either live with this (no resizing) or bolt on a heavy workaround that breaks on multi-monitor setups or high-DPI displays.
[...]
Rython solves this with a lean, direct WinAPI approach:
[...]
- **Resize handles** — 8 px invisible CSS strips at every edge and corner. On `mousedown` they call `window.pywebview.api.start_drag(mode)`, which issues a `SendMessage(hwnd, WM_NCLBUTTONDOWN, HT_*)` hit-test directly into the window message queue. Windows then handles the resize natively — no JS polling, no coordinate math, no jitter.
[...]
- **Title
[...]
bar drag** — a daemon thread polls
[...]
SetWindowPos
[...]
- **Snap shortcuts** — `Alt+←`, `Alt+→`, `Alt+↑`, `Alt+↓` call `snap_window()`, which queries the correct monitor's work area via `MonitorFromPoint` / `GetMonitorInfoW` and clamps the window to the target bounds — correctly handling taskbars, multi-monitor layouts, and different DPI scales.
[...]
- **HWND cache** — the window handle is resolved once via `FindWindowW` and cached, so every subsequent WinAPI call is a direct pointer dereference with no search overhead.
[...]
- **Style preservation** —
[...]
are saved at
[...]
SetWindowPos`
[...]
preventing the invisible-border artefact that appears when pywebview's internal style patching conflicts with a resize operation.
[...]
The net result is a frameless window that behaves identically to a native one: resizable from all edges and corners, draggable, snappable, maximisable, and correctly clipped to the monitor work area — without any visible frame, title bar chrome, or OS decoration.
[...]
- Frameless window with fully custom title bar
- Resize from all 8 edges and corners via WinAPI hit-testing
- Title-bar drag via daemon-thread `SetWindowPos` loop
- Snap: `Alt+←` left-half · `Alt+→` right-half · `Alt+↓` centred · `Alt+↑` maximise ↔ restore
- Double-click title bar to maximise / restore
- Traffic-light close and minimise buttons
[...]
`
- macOS-style modal dialogs
[...]
`
- Dark / light theme persisted
[...]
localStorage`
- SQLite ready — commented scaffold
[...]
routes
- `Window
[...]
OS-level
[...]
file/folder

results.title: Transparent window DOES NOT work! [windows-11] · Issue #1611 · r0x0r/pywebview
results.author: ccocks
results.text:
# Issue: r0x0r/pywebview #1611

- Repository: r0x0r/pywebview | Build GUI for your Python program with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS | 6K stars | Python

## Transparent window DOES NOT work! [windows-11]

- Author: [@ccocks](https://github.com/ccocks)
- State: closed (completed)
- Created: 2025-03-14T22:59:35Z
- Updated: 2025-10-24T17:05:48Z
- Closed: 2025-03-24T08:47:18Z
- Closed by: [@r0x0r](https://github.com/r0x0r)

The background appears as my windows11 theme color, not what's behind the window

---

### Timeline

**@r0x0r** commented · Mar 24, 2025 at 8:47am

> It is a known issue and is a limitation of webview2. Nothing can be done about it, until transparency support is added to webview2

**r0x0r** closed this · Mar 24, 2025 at 8:47am

**@r0x0r** commented · Mar 24, 2025 at 12:07pm

> Sorry my bad. Transparency is actually supported, but became broken after window shadow was introduced. The problem is that with transparent window Webview2 is not able to process any mouse clicks.

**@ccocks** commented · Apr 1, 2025 at 3:56pm · Author

> @r0x0r Workarounds?

**@r0x0r** commented · Apr 4, 2025 at 9:02pm

> @blazgocompany the workaround is to get it fixed. I had considered it unfixable, but then I discovered a way to do it here https://github.com/Faksimile/WebView2-Click-Through 
> I have made some progress by implementing WndProc hook, but not much more than that I am afraid. The current progress can be seen in the `winforms-transparency` branch. WndProc is working, but the rest of changes are just experimentations

**@heyManNice** commented · May 10, 2025 at 6:44pm

> > Sorry my bad. Transparency is actually supported, but became broken after window shadow was introduced. The problem is that with transparent window Webview2 is not able to process any mouse clicks.
> 
> This doesn't seem to be a WebView2 issue but rather a WinForms-related behavior. I noticed that when ```transparent=True``` and ```frameless=True```, removing ```self.TransparencyKey = Color.FromArgb(255,255,0,0)``` from line 256 of webview/platforms/winforms.py allows WebView2 to be transparent while still responding to mouse events. Hope this helps!

**@r0x0r** commented · May 13, 2025 at 8:17pm

> @heyManNice TransparencyKey is for there for a reason. It makes background color transparent. Without that line background is red

**@heyManNice** commented · May 14, 2025 at 6:46am

> @r0x0r With ```transparent=True``` and ```frameless=True```, the background color I got was not red but transparent. I have posted a demonstration video in a new issue. #1634

**@heyManNice** commented · May 16, 2025 at 4:28pm · edited

> @r0x0r Sorry, I overlooked one detail: After creating the window, it must be hidden first and then shown again to achieve transparency; otherwise, it will appear red. You can pass ```hidden=True``` when calling create_window, or execute ```window.hide() window.show()```. After testing, this only works on the pywebview5.4 branch and has no effect on the master branch. On pywebview5.4, ```shadow=False``` is required.

**@r0x0r** commented · May 17, 2025 at 6:58am

> @heyManNice That is very interesting. I got it working using your hack. I tried to incorporate it into the codebase, but it works half of the time. For example if you attach a debugger and break at a breakpoint before window is shown, window won't be transparent.

**@r0x0r** commented · May 17, 2025 at 7:11am

> I pushed a fix to the `6.0` branch. I looks like it works pretty robustly now.

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 12, 2025 at 8:51pm · Author

> Whew thanks.

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 13, 2025 at 8:48pm · Author

> @r0x0r, Can you tell me how to use it, I see that it is currently on 5.4 but you say you have changes on 6.0, how to install and use that?

**@xianfei** commented · Jun 14, 2025 at 5:17pm

> Just change here, it works well on 5.4.
> 
> [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/749bd4a2-d1a4-47e6-9762-e136a4e786cd]
> 
> Remove this two line:
> 
> ```
> self.BackColor = Color.FromArgb(255,255,0,0)
> self.TransparencyKey = Color.FromArgb(255,255,0,0)
> ```
> 
> and add this line:
> 
> ```
> windll.user32.SetWindowLongW(self.Handle.ToInt32(), -20,
> windll.user32.GetWindowLongW(self.Handle.ToInt32(), -20) | 0x80000)
> ```

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 14, 2025 at 8:06pm · Author

> Hmm... Where does this go, @xianfei ?

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 14, 2025 at 8:23pm · Author

> Never mind, I found it.... Thanks

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 14, 2025 at 8:28pm · Author

> One problem:
> I was trying to add a blur effect using `backdrop-filter`
> But it doesn't work:
> 
> [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ccbba5d-7dbb-4a67-b41a-1a4786aaee17]
> 
> Does pywebview not support it or is it something to do with the rendering

**@chrissantamaria** commented · Jun 21, 2025 at 4:26pm · edited

> 👋 running into this as well on Windows 11 24H2 (`26100.4349`) with `edgechromium`, Python 3.12.0. So far I've tested `examples/transparent.py` on:
> 
> - `master` (currently `8e88814`): no transparency or mouse events
> - `5.4`: no transparency or mouse events
> - `5.4` with [xianfei's changes](https://github.com/r0x0r/pywebview/issues/1611#issuecomment-2972891556): transparency works, but no mouse events
> - `6.0` (currently `2bb808f`): no transparency, but working mouse events
> 
> On `6.0` I've tried a few variations of calling `window.hide()` + `window.show()` at different timings with no luck.

**@r0x0r** commented · Jun 22, 2025 at 8:33pm

> I got it working on my setup, nor I have any idea why @xianfei fix works at all, so I am afraid I have no answers for this issue.

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 22, 2025 at 9:07pm · Author

> ok, but what about background blurring? Is that an issue or out of scope?

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 22, 2025 at 9:08pm · Author

> > I got it working on my setup, nor I have any idea why [@xianfei](https://github.com/xianfei) fix works at all, so I am afraid I have no answers for this iss
> 
> Could you show your `pip show pywebview`?

**@r0x0r** commented · Jun 24, 2025 at 7:13am

> @blazgocompany I recreated a clean Python env and tested it succesfully against the latest 6.0 branch. `pip show pywebview` has nothing interesting
> 
> ```
> Location: C:\Users\roman\AppData\Local\Packages\PythonSoftwareFoundation.Python.3.13_qbz5n2kfra8p0\LocalCache\local-packages\Python313\site-packages
> Requires: bottle, proxy_tools, pythonnet, typing_extensions
> Required-by:
> ```
> 
> I have not looked into background blurring. macOS has vibrancy effect support, but that is a bit different

**@ccocks** commented · Jun 24, 2025 at 1:53pm · Author

> Ok, so I guess the best way to add a blur is to do it dynamically through js....

**@Jai-wei** commented · Jul 20, 2025 at 10:01am

> > 只需更改此处，它在 5.4 上运行良好。
> > 
> > [Image: Image | https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/749bd4a2-d1a4-47e6-9762-e136a4e786cd]
> > 
> > 删除这两行：
> > 
> > ```
> > self.BackColor = Color.FromArgb(255,255,0,0)
> > self.TransparencyKey = Color.FromArgb(255,255,0,0)
> > ```
> > 
> > 并添加以下行：
> > 
> > ```
> > windll.user32.SetWindowLongW(self.Handle.ToInt32(), -20,
> > windll.user32.GetWindowLongW(self.Handle.ToInt32(), -20) | 0x80000)
> > ```
> 
> good job!

**@heyManNice** commented · Oct 24, 2025 at 5:05pm

> I've been working on a Win32 application recently and found that using:
> 
> ```
> SetWindowLong(hwnd, GWL_EXSTYLE, GetWindowLong(hwnd, GWL_EXSTYLE) | WS_EX_LAYERED);
> SetLayeredWindowAttributes(hwnd, RGB(0, 255, 1), 255, LWA_COLORKEY);
> ```
> 
> makes the window background transparent while still allowing mouse interaction.
> 
> One important detail: when using LWA_COLORKEY, the red component must be 0, green can be any value, and blue must be non-zero. Otherwise, the window won't respond to mouse events.
